summary: 0045's embedded-delivery ruling: an embedded kind now takes a locus-optional kind-fact row (guidance/cite reach the lock), and explain gains the bare-kind form to narrate it pre-member.

edit.description: `kindFactRow` (~294-299) drops the `facts.locus.kind === "embedded"` throw-guard — it builds the row exactly as it already does for a nested-file kind (`governs` undefined), so `guidance`/`cite` (already columns on the row, 314-315) reach the lock for a custom embedded kind. `unitKindsInPlay` (~357-364), whose filter was 'every locus but embedded', becomes an identity now that embedded also takes a row — retire it and feed `kindsInPlay(harness)` straight to the `kinds:` row family at its one call site (~879).

The 'Kinds in play' section header (~423-425) and the test `an embedded member neither projects nor takes a kind-fact row` (~441-449) assert `declarations.kinds` excludes the embedded kind by name — the exact assumption this ruling reverses. Rewrite: the embedded kind's row now appears in `declarations.kinds` (name present, `governs_root`/`governs_glob`/`commitment` absent) while the kind still emits no standalone file projection.

`embedded_features_by_kind`'s doc comment (~1085-1099) states an embedded kind 'carries no kind-fact row' as the reason it needs its own admission-driven seam into the corpus. That premise is now false for a custom embedded kind's guidance/cite (its row exists), though discovery/admission is unchanged — still driven by `templates`/`admit`, never the row. Correct the comment; no behavior change here.

Add the bare-kind surface: a `Species::Kind(&str)` variant: `resolve` (~181-214) matches a bare name against the kind set `explain_target` (~1541) already assembles (built-in `contracts` keys plus `custom_rows`-derived custom/embedded kind names) once a member/requirement match doesn't win — extend the existing member-vs-requirement `Ambiguous` triad by construction, no new precedence question. `explain` (~241) dispatches `Species::Kind` to a new narration reading the resolved `CustomKind`'s (or built-in's) `guidance`/`cite` — the pre-member counterpart to the per-member governing-contract narration `why_impl` already runs for an embedded *member* (that path is unchanged; a member's guidance already surfaces once its kind's row exists, no fix needed there). Update `explain`'s doc comment (~216-218) to name the kind namespace, matching contract.md's amended 'Read verbs' line.

The `Explain` command's doc comment and `target` field doc (~187-201) enumerate member/requirement/leaf-address namespaces and their qualified spellings (`member:`/`requirement:`/`address:`); add the kind namespace (and a `kind:` qualified spelling, for symmetry with the other three) to both.

New tests alongside `explain_narrates_kind_guidance_in_governing_contract` (~604) and `explain_omits_governing_contract_guidance_when_absent` (~661): `explain <kind-name>` on a kind carrying `guidance` narrates it with no member of that kind in the corpus (the pre-member surface); a kind absent guidance narrates cleanly with no spurious text; a custom *embedded* kind's row-carried guidance reaches this surface end to end (proves the sdk/src/declarations.ts row change lands, not just the Rust dispatch).

tests.asserts: an embedded kind's declared guidance/cite ride a locus-absent kind-fact row in `declarations.kinds`, the kind still emitting no standalone projection
`explain <kind>` narrates a kind's declared guidance/cite with no member instance present, cleanly omitting narration when guidance is absent
a custom embedded kind's guidance, declared in the SDK, is narrated by `explain <kind>` after a full emit/lock/read round trip

acceptance: A custom embedded kind's declared guidance/cite survives SDK → lock → `CustomKind` reconstruction as a locus-optional row (inheriting the existing bare-name collision gate), and `explain <kind-name>` narrates it before any member of that kind exists; `pnpm --dir sdk test` and `cargo test` both green.
notes: 20a6f54 supplies its own 'Routing bound for the deriving entry' checklist, followed here. admissibility.rs (effective_kinds/local_locus/registration_locus/governs_collision) and compose.rs's `resolve_kind_units` `(_, _, None)` branch already tolerate `governs: None` generically (the nested-file path) — no functional Rust fix expected beyond the stale comment; confirm with a green `cargo test` first. Templates-rider and a new row family are foreclosed by the ruling itself. scoped at 20a6f54.

summary: Packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing) and the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json channel (channel 3).

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) is now proven live, not merely wired: the v0.0.11 tag fired it and its publish smoke gate went green (b6dc0a3's commit body).

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json) — distribution.md's v0.1 launch gate: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes.
notes: Park re-tested on disk at 01ba369: release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim (lines 7-9). The lockstep clause carried here since ac40c72/b676656 is resolved, dropped: da77c60 walked Cargo.toml 0.1.0→0.0.11 to match npm's cadence directly (no v0.1-tag flip), b6dc0a3 fixed the lockfile mismatch, and tag v0.0.11→da77c60 fired release.yml with a green smoke gate per its own commit body — channel 2 is proven live, not just wired. scoped 01ba369.

summary: 0045 also frees guidance onto a field declaration, no predicate — deferred: none of the six promoted built-in notes targets a single field, so there is no consumer yet

gate.reason: 0045's Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level, but the SDK's only per-field metadata today is a clause's `field:` target (predicate-bound) or `EdgeField` (edge-typed only) — no 'field declaration' list a bare advisory could attach to. None of the six promoted notes (GUIDANCE-BUILTIN-NOTES-PROMOTE) target a single field, inventing the mechanism now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it ('An export earns its consumer', engineering.md). Revive when a field wants counsel no clause carries.

edit.description: Anticipated: a field-name-keyed guidance fact on `KindFacts`, analogous to `EdgeField`'s field-name-keyed list (196) but for arbitrary fields — shape unset; no field in any built-in kind wants one yet.
Anticipated: a `KindFactRow` column (or sibling row) carrying per-field guidance/cite, following the `to_table`/`from_table` pattern GUIDANCE-KIND-SDK-LOCK-FACT establishes for the kind-level pair.

Anticipated: `emit()`'s per-property `description` (156-172) folds field-declaration guidance in alongside a clause's, the same 'join into one description' discipline `push_description` (316-329) already runs for several guided clauses on one field.
Anticipated: field-level narration extends `narrate_governing_contract` (374-402) or a sibling to print a field's declared guidance.

acceptance: A field declaring `guidance` (no predicate) surfaces it via `schema` hover and `explain`, never gating — provable once a real kind needs one.
notes: 0045 Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level; this waits for a driver, the same 'no consumer' bar prior deferred entries in this queue's history have held to. scoped at 946e303.
